creator: conrads@cox.net
text:
On Wed, 21 Mar 2012 13:48:28 +0600
Muhammad Moinur Rahman <5u623l20@gmail.com> wrote:

> It's not about deserving. It's about working with the community and
> contributing to the community. It's about leveraging the work
> pressure of the FreeBSD committers who are working to provide us a
> robust stable *NIX variant.
> 
> I will leave it upon the committers to decide.
> 
> Happy porting. :D
> 
> Regards,
> Muhammad

Well, the reason I suggested you go ahead and stay on as maintainer is
that, upon taking a closer look at the package again the last couple of
days, I'm tempted to make some major revisions in how certain aspects
of the installation are handled, such as using the install.sh script
included with the package, which, among other things, prompts the user
re: a license agreement.  Not sure I like the idea of simply
circumventing that and doing our own install procedure instead, you
know?

I just don't want the port to be held up indefinitely while I go back
and basically rethink the whole approach to use and try to decide what
would be best.  I told Wayne Cheng <wcheng@softintegration.com>, the guy
who I've been corresponding with there and who was instrumental in
getting an updated package for FreeBSD 9.0 released, that it would
probably only take a day or two for the new port to be reviewed and
committed.  He's probably wondering what's going on.

So, if you'd like to retain maintainership of the port, that's fine.  I
wouldn't have even tried to "usurp" it from you if I had known.  My
ports tree was out-of-date just enough when I first submitted the
patch, that it looked like the port was currently still unmaintained,
otherwise I never would have presumed to try to stake a claim on it, so
to speak.

Thanks for all your help and hard work!

Conrad

-- 
Conrad J. Sabatier
conrads@cox.net
